Code inspection: verifier.py line 93 checks r3d.get('size_bytes', 0) >= 30000. This threshold ensures the 3D view is not blank/empty and is a reasonable sanity check for a JPEG render of 3D interior content (typical renders are 50KB-500KB+, making 30KB a low threshold). Confirmed in verifier.py comment line 14: '3D view size > 30 KB (real rendered image)'

Code inspection: verifier.py line 109 checks rblue.get('size_bytes', 0) >= 10000. This threshold ensures the floor plan is not blank/empty and is a reasonable sanity check (typical floor plan images are 20KB-200KB+, making 10KB a low threshold). Confirmed in verifier.py comment line 16: 'floor_plan.jpg size > 10 KB (real floor plan)'

privileged_info_summary: All metadata claims in task.json are verified. The task uses DreamPlan home design software and its built-in Contemporary House sample project. Output files must be saved to: Desktop/renovation_3d_view.jpg, Desktop/renovation_floor_plan.jpg, and Documents/renovation_proposal.dpn. The verification process checks file existence, modification timestamps, and enforces minimum file sizes (30 KB for 3D view, 10 KB for floor plan) to ensure valid rendered output. The result is written to C:\Users\Docker\renovation_material_package_result.json with task start timestamp from C:\Users\Docker\task_start_ts_renovation_material.txt.
